title: Constitutional Law of India
author: J.N. Pandey
subject: Constitutional Law
description: Comprehensive coverage of the Constitution of India for UP PCS-J. Includes Preamble, Fundamental Rights (Part III), Directive Principles (Part IV), Union & State Executive, Judiciary, Emergency Provisions, and Constitutional Amendments. Authored by J.N. Pandey, the standard text for Judicial Services examination.

title: Code of Civil Procedure, 1908
author: C.K. Takwani
subject: Civil Procedure
description: Authoritative commentary on the CPC 1908 by C.K. Takwani. Covers jurisdiction of courts, pleadings, res judicata, interim orders, appeals, revisions, execution of decrees, and special proceedings. Essential for Civil Judge (Junior Division) examination.

title: Law of Evidence
author: Batuklal
subject: Evidence Law
description: Batuklal's definitive treatise on the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam 2023 (replacing Indian Evidence Act 1872). Covers relevancy of facts, admissions, confessions, dying declarations, expert opinion, burden of proof, estoppel, witnesses, and examination of witnesses. Core text for UP PCS-J.

title: Indian Penal Law - IPC & BNS
author: Ratanlal & Dhirajlal
subject: Criminal Law
description: Ratanlal & Dhirajlal's classic work on Indian criminal law covering both IPC 1860 and Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023. Includes general principles, offences against body, property, state, public tranquility, and general exceptions. The gold standard for Judicial Services criminal law preparation.

title: Indian Contract Act, 1872
author: Avtar Singh
subject: Contract Law
description: Avtar Singh's authoritative text on the Indian Contract Act 1872. Covers formation, consideration, capacity, free consent, void agreements, performance, discharge, remedies for breach, and specific contracts including indemnity, guarantee, bailment, pledge, and agency.

title: Jurisprudence and Legal Theory
author: V.D. Mahajan
subject: Jurisprudence
description: V.D. Mahajan's comprehensive treatise on Jurisprudence covering schools of law (Analytical, Historical, Sociological, Philosophical), concepts of law, rights, duties, liability, ownership, possession, and legal reasoning. Essential for UP PCS-J mains examination.

title: Law of Torts and Consumer Protection
author: R.K. Bangia
subject: Law of Torts
description: R.K. Bangia's text on tortious liability covering negligence, nuisance, defamation, trespass, strict and absolute liability, vicarious liability, torts against person and property, and Consumer Protection Act 2019. Includes landmark Supreme Court judgments.

title: Transfer of Property Act, 1882
author: R.K. Sinha
subject: Transfer of Property
description: R.K. Sinha's commentary on the Transfer of Property Act 1882. Covers sale, mortgage, lease, exchange, gift, actionable claims, and doctrine of election, feeding by estoppel, and lis pendens. Critical for UP PCS-J Civil Judge examination.

title: Limitation Act, 1963 & Court Fees
author: J.N. Pandey
subject: Limitation & Court Fees
description: J.N. Pandey's focused study on the Limitation Act 1963 and U.P. Court Fees Act. Covers computation of limitation period, acknowledgment, part-payment, legal disability, condonation of delay, and court fee valuation. High-weightage subject in UP PCS-J.

title: Local Laws of Uttar Pradesh
author: LexPrep AI Team
subject: U.P. Local Laws
description: Comprehensive coverage of U.P. local laws for UP PCS-J: U.P. Revenue Code 2006, U.P. Municipalities Act 1916, U.P. Panchayat Raj Act 1947, U.P. Urban Planning and Development Act 1973, and U.P. Land Reforms Act. Exclusive to UP PCS-J syllabus.
